title: Pecluma cupreolepis
intro: Pecluma cupreolepis, also known as the copper shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Pecluma cupreolepis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pecluma cupreolepis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health issues, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
uses: Pecluma cupreolepis is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
